title: Evaluate build-time timestamp as GNSS epoch fallback
description: User-proposed design idea: use firmware build-time stamps, and possibly board-provided build metadata, as an epoch/freshness floor for GNSS time handling before a real GNSS lock is available. Evaluate whether this is safe for LICHEN time synchronization, replay windows, logs, certificate/trust validation, and UX. This is software/design work first; any real GNSS validation must be split into a hardware follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether build-time timestamps may be used as a time floor or GNSS epoch hint, identifies trust/security limits and stale firmware behavior, specifies required firmware metadata format if accepted, and creates a hardware validation follow-up if physical GNSS behavior must be checked.
notes:
Decision: accepted with limits. Firmware build-time metadata and optional board provision metadata MAY be used as the effective epoch floor for GNSS/date-time freshness, but MUST NOT be treated as authoritative wall-clock time. The provider policy remains: effective_epoch_floor = max(firmware_build_epoch, valid_board_provision_epoch_if_present). Time samples below that floor, including cold-start/default GNSS epochs, zero local-client time, stale network time, or manual/static mistakes, MUST NOT establish wall-clock validity. GNSS position and GNSS time remain independently valid: a position fix can be retained while fix_time_unix_valid/wall_clock_valid stays false if GNSS time is below the floor.

Security/trust limits: build/provision epochs only reject impossible stale samples; they do not prove freshness for replay windows, certificates, OSCORE/EDHOC trust, or logs. Security-sensitive freshness still needs an accepted time source with source/trust metadata or monotonic/session-specific freshness mechanisms. Stale firmware behavior: production firmware must carry deterministic SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH or explicit release epoch metadata and fail production builds if missing; developer builds may use a generated timestamp only in an explicit non-reproducible mode that is marked/tested and not confused with release metadata. Board provision epochs must be integrity-protected with board identity/settings, non-zero, at or after build epoch, within the deterministic provision lead bound, and recoverable/clearable if poisoned.

Required implementation metadata: expose firmware build epoch, build epoch source/mode, optional provision epoch validity/value, effective epoch floor, last rejection reason including below_epoch_floor, and source class/name for accepted or rejected samples. project-LICHEN-2067.1 remains the no-hardware implementation follow-up for SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH/release epoch wiring and native_sim/Twister tests. Created hardware follow-up project-LICHEN-xj8k.1 for physical GNSS validation.
close_reason: Completed decision: build/provision timestamps are accepted only as an epoch floor/freshness lower bound, not authoritative clock time; implementation and hardware validation follow-ups are filed.
